agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"A logistics platform introducing automated route optimization for drivers at shift start, with dynamic recalculation triggers, customer notification windows, vehicle class enforcement, and configurable stop duration. This spans multiple bounded contexts: route planning, fleet/driver management, delivery lifecycle, and customer notifications.","bounded_context":"Route Optimization — but this feature crosses into Fleet Management (vehicle class licensing), Delivery Management (delivery lifecycle states), and Customer Notification (proximity alerts). The primary bounded context is Route Planning, which consumes events from adjacent contexts.","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Optimized Route","definition":"An ordered sequence of delivery stops assigned to a driver, calculated to minimize total travel time/distance within their zone, respecting all constraints (vehicle class, zone boundaries)."},{"term":"Zone","definition":"A geographic area used to partition deliveries and assign them to drivers. A driver is associated with exactly one zone per shift."},{"term":"Shift","definition":"A bounded work period for a driver, starting when they clock in and ending when they clock out or complete all assigned stops. Route assignment is triggered at shift start."},{"term":"Pending Delivery","definition":"A delivery that has not yet been picked up or is awaiting assignment to a driver route. Only pending deliveries in the driver's zone are candidates for route inclusion."},{"term":"Vehicle Class","definition":"A classification of the vehicle a driver is licensed to operate (e.g., motorcycle, van, truck, freight). Determines which deliveries can be physically assigned to the driver based on shipment size/type."},{"term":"Licensed Vehicle Class","definition":"The vehicle class(es) a driver is certified and authorized to operate. Acts as a hard constraint on delivery assignment."},{"term":"Route Recalculation","definition":"The process of regenerating the optimized route in response to a trigger event: new delivery added, delivery marked failed, or driver deviation."},{"term":"Driver Deviation","definition":"When a driver's actual GPS position or stop sequence diverges from the suggested optimized route beyond a defined threshold."},{"term":"Next 3 Stops Window","definition":"A sliding window of the upcoming three delivery stops on a driver's active route. Customers whose deliveries fall within this window receive a proximity notification."},{"term":"Failed Delivery","definition":"A delivery that could not be completed (e.g., recipient unavailable, access denied). Triggers route recalculation to remove this stop and re-optimize remaining stops."},{"term":"Stop Duration","definition":"The estimated time a driver spends at each delivery stop. Defaults to 4 minutes but is overridable by the operations team on a per-zone basis."},{"term":"Operations Team","definition":"Internal stakeholders with authority to configure zone-level parameters such as stop duration overrides."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"When a driver starts their shift, the system MUST automatically generate an optimized route from all pending deliveries in the driver's assigned zone.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"This is an operational workflow trigger — the optimization algorithm and zone assignment are policy decisions that could change (e.g., pre-shift route generation, multi-zone assignment).","testable_assertion":"Given a driver starts a shift in zone X, the system produces a route containing all pending deliveries in zone X within a defined SLA (e.g., < 30 seconds)."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"A driver MUST NOT be assigned a delivery whose shipment requirements exceed their licensed vehicle class.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"This is a safety and regulatory constraint. Assigning a freight shipment to a motorcycle driver is physically impossible and potentially illegal. Violating this corrupts system integrity and creates real-world danger.","testable_assertion":"For every delivery on a driver's route, delivery.requiredVehicleClass is compatible with driver.licensedVehicleClass. No exceptions."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"The route MUST be recalculated when a new pending delivery is added to the driver's zone.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"This is an operational policy for route freshness. The business could decide to batch recalculations or defer to driver discretion.","testable_assertion":"When a new delivery is created in zone X and a driver in zone X has an active route, the route is recalculated to include the new delivery."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"The route MUST be recalculated when a delivery on the route is marked as failed.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Operational policy — the business could alternatively just skip the stop without full recalculation.","testable_assertion":"When a delivery on an active route transitions to 'failed' status, the route is recalculated excluding that delivery."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"The route MUST be recalculated when the driver manually deviates from the suggested route.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Operational policy for maintaining route relevance. Could be relaxed to only recalculate on request.","testable_assertion":"When a driver's actual position or stop sequence deviates from the planned route beyond a defined threshold, a recalculation is triggered automatically."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"Customers MUST be notified when their delivery enters the 'next 3 stops' window on the driver's route.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Notification policy — the window size (3 stops) is a business preference, not a physical constraint.","testable_assertion":"When a delivery moves into position 1, 2, or 3 on the active route, the associated customer receives exactly one proximity notification."},{"id":"BR-7","rule":"The default estimated stop duration is 4 minutes. The operations team can override this value on a per-zone basis.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"This is a configurable operational parameter, explicitly designed to be overridden.","testable_assertion":"The route calculation uses zone-specific stop duration if an override exists; otherwise defaults to 4 minutes. Override values are persisted per zone."},{"id":"BR-8","rule":"A driver can only be assigned deliveries within their assigned zone during a shift.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Zone boundaries are a fundamental partitioning constraint. Cross-zone assignment would violate delivery ownership, SLA calculations, and operational accountability.","testable_assertion":"Every delivery on a driver's route belongs to the same zone as the driver's active shift assignment."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"A driver starts their shift and there are 10 pending deliveries in their zone","when":"The shift is activated","then":"The system generates an optimized route containing all 10 deliveries, ordered for minimum travel time/distance, within an acceptable latency threshold"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"A driver with an active route in zone A","when":"A new delivery is added to zone A","then":"The driver's route is recalculated to include the new delivery in the optimal position, and the driver is informed of the route change"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"A driver with an active route containing delivery D","when":"Delivery D is marked as failed","then":"The route is recalculated excluding delivery D, remaining stops are re-optimized, and the driver sees the updated route"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"A driver following their optimized route","when":"The driver deviates from the suggested route (skips a stop or takes a different path)","then":"The system detects the deviation and recalculates the route from the driver's current position"},{"id":"AC-5","given":"A customer's delivery is at position 4 or higher on the route","when":"The route advances such that their delivery moves to position 3, 2, or 1","then":"The customer receives a single notification indicating their delivery is approaching"},{"id":"AC-6","given":"A motorcycle-licensed driver starts their shift","when":"There are pending deliveries requiring a freight vehicle class in their zone","then":"Those freight deliveries are excluded from the driver's route and remain unassigned (or assigned to an eligible driver)"},{"id":"AC-7","given":"The operations team sets the stop duration for zone B to 6 minutes","when":"A route is calculated for a driver in zone B","then":"The route uses 6 minutes per stop for ETA calculations instead of the default 4 minutes"},{"id":"AC-8","given":"A driver starts their shift and there are zero pending deliveries in their zone","when":"The shift is activated","then":"The system assigns an empty route and monitors for new deliveries to trigger route creation"},{"id":"AC-9","given":"A route recalculation is triggered (new delivery, failure, or deviation)","when":"The driver has already completed some stops","then":"Only remaining uncompleted stops are included in the recalculated route; completed stops are not revisited"},{"id":"AC-10","given":"A customer has already been notified they are in the next-3-stops window","when":"A route recalculation moves their delivery outside the next-3-stops window","then":"The system handles this gracefully — the customer is not sent a contradictory notification (business must define policy here)"}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Multiple drivers in the same zone start shifts simultaneously","expected_behavior":"Deliveries must be partitioned across drivers — not duplicated. The optimization must account for all active drivers in the zone to avoid assigning the same delivery to multiple drivers."},{"scenario":"A recalculation is triggered while another recalculation is already in progress","expected_behavior":"The system must handle concurrent recalculation triggers gracefully — either queue them, debounce, or use the latest state. Must not produce a stale or inconsistent route."},{"scenario":"A new delivery is added to a zone with no active drivers","expected_behavior":"The delivery remains in pending state. When a driver starts a shift in that zone, it is included in their initial route."},{"scenario":"A driver completes all deliveries and a new one arrives","expected_behavior":"The route is recalculated with the single new delivery, and the driver receives the updated route."},{"scenario":"The only delivery in the next-3-stops window is marked as failed","expected_behavior":"Route recalculates. A different delivery may now enter the window, triggering a new customer notification. The original customer whose delivery failed should receive a separate failure notification (not a proximity one)."},{"scenario":"Driver deviates but returns to the original route","expected_behavior":"If recalculation was triggered on deviation, the new route may differ from the original. The system should not enter a recalculation loop if the driver is near the planned path."},{"scenario":"Operations team changes stop duration mid-route","expected_behavior":"The spec does not clarify whether in-progress routes should use the new duration. Recommendation: apply new duration on next recalculation, not retroactively."},{"scenario":"A delivery requires a vehicle class that no currently active driver is licensed for","expected_behavior":"The delivery remains pending/unassigned. The system should surface this to dispatch or operations as an alert — it cannot be silently dropped."},{"scenario":"Driver's vehicle class changes mid-shift (e.g., switches vehicles)","expected_behavior":"Not addressed in spec. If allowed, a recalculation should enforce the new vehicle class constraints immediately."},{"scenario":"Customer notification sent, then route recalculation changes ETA significantly","expected_behavior":"Business must decide if a follow-up notification with updated ETA is required or if the original notification stands."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Route optimization directly reduces fuel costs, driver overtime, and failed delivery rates. Dynamic recalculation minimizes wasted trips. Incorrect vehicle class assignment could result in regulatory fines, damaged goods claims, or insurance liability.","operational":"Frequent recalculations under high delivery volume could strain system resources. The 4-minute default stop duration affects ETA accuracy — poor estimates erode dispatcher and customer trust. Multi-driver zone partitioning is an NP-hard optimization problem at scale.","user":"Customers gain transparency via the next-3-stops notification, reducing 'where is my package' inquiries. Drivers benefit from automated route planning but may resist if recalculations feel disruptive or override their local knowledge.","risk":"Vehicle class enforcement is safety-critical — failure here has legal and physical consequences. Excessive recalculations could create a poor driver experience and GPS/notification fatigue for customers. Deviation detection threshold is undefined — too sensitive triggers constant recalc, too loose renders it useless.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Drivers","impact":"Primary users — receive automated routes, must follow or deviate. Route quality directly affects their efficiency, earnings, and job satisfaction. Frequent recalculations may be disruptive."},{"group":"Customers","impact":"Receive proximity notifications improving delivery predictability. Poor route optimization or excessive recalculation could lead to inaccurate ETAs and notification spam."},{"group":"Operations Team","impact":"Gains zone-level configuration controls (stop duration). Needs visibility into unassignable deliveries and route efficiency metrics."},{"group":"Dispatch / Fleet Management","impact":"Vehicle class enforcement affects fleet utilization planning. Must handle edge cases where no eligible driver exists for a delivery."},{"group":"Finance / Business Leadership","impact":"ROI depends on measurable reduction in fuel costs, delivery times, and failed delivery rates. Needs KPIs to track optimization effectiveness."}]},"confidence":{"level":72,"reasoning":"The spec is relatively well-defined for a product description. Core business rules are explicit and unambiguous. However, several operational details are undefined: deviation detection threshold, multi-driver partitioning strategy, notification behavior on route changes, and concurrency handling. These are implementation-critical but not domain-ambiguous — they are standard logistics concerns that need product decisions.","high_confidence_areas":["Vehicle class enforcement as a hard invariant","Route recalculation triggers (3 explicit triggers well-defined)","Customer notification on next-3-stops window","Stop duration default and override mechanism","Zone-based delivery assignment"],"low_confidence_areas":["Deviation detection threshold and mechanism not defined","Multi-driver delivery partitioning strategy within a zone not specified","Notification behavior when recalculation invalidates a previously sent notification","Whether mid-route configuration changes (stop duration) apply immediately or on next recalc","How the system handles unassignable deliveries (no eligible driver)"],"assumptions":["A driver is assigned to exactly one zone per shift","Zone assignment is determined before or at shift start and does not change mid-shift","Vehicle class compatibility is a simple hierarchy or category match, not a complex matrix","Route optimization means minimizing travel time/distance (not other objectives like priority)","The next-3-stops notification is sent exactly once per delivery entering the window, not repeatedly"]},"escalations":[],"rejection_reasons":[]}
